deltaNote: vs 2023 trough (3.4%); Feb revised down 130K to net -92K — first monthly payroll loss since 2020

label: US 10Y Treasury Yield
source: Federal Reserve H.15 / FRED DGS10, Apr 10 2026
deltaNote: Apr 10 settle at 4.31% — down from 4.46% Apr 4 peak but elevated; core CPI miss (2.6% vs 2.7% forecast) provided mild bond relief; S&P +3% for week; inflation risk premium persists ahead of Apr 14 WEO and Apr 29–30 FOMC

label: US Consumer Confidence
deltaNote: Marginal improvement from Feb (91.0); Expectations Index 70.9 — 14th consecutive month below 80 recession-warning threshold

label: Michigan Consumer Sentiment (Apr Prelim.)
deltaNote: ALL-TIME RECORD LOW — first below 50 since June 2022; below 1980 energy crisis low (51.7). Year-ahead inflation expectations 4.8% (from 3.8% — largest one-month jump in survey history). 98% of surveys conducted before Apr 7 Iran ceasefire.

label: VIX (Market Fear Index)
delta: Pulling back from Apr 9 spike (29–31) as markets process CPI core miss (2.6% — below forecast)
deltaNote: Apr 10 stabilization: core CPI 0.1pp below forecast + Iran ceasefire holding partially eased fear. BUT Michigan sentiment 47.6 (all-time low) and year-ahead inflation expectations 4.8% (record monthly jump) keep elevated. S&P +3% for week. FOMC Apr 29–30 critical stagflation decision.

label: US Avg Effective Tariff Rate
contestNote: Apr 9: additional 50% tariff on China brings total US rate on Chinese goods to ~104% — highest since 1930s Smoot-Hawley era. Combined with Apr 2 metal expansion (50%) and pending pharma tariffs (100%), overall effective rate reaches highest since 1940s

label: China GDP Forecast 2025
contestNote: Down 0.6pp from Jan 2025 forecast; property crisis, tariff headwinds weigh on growth

label: Global Policy Uncertainty Index
source: Baker-Bloom-Davis / FRED; University of Michigan Apr 10 2026
delta: Surging — triple shock: Iran war energy, US-China 104%/84% tariff near-embargo, Michigan sentiment record low
deltaNote: Apr 10: Michigan Consumer Sentiment 47.6 (all-time record low); year-ahead inflation expectations 4.8% (largest monthly jump in survey history). JPMorgan recession probability 65%, Moody's Analytics ~50%, Goldman 35%. IMF Spring Meetings Apr 13–18; WEO Apr 14 will show growth markdowns ~0.8pp. FOMC Apr 29–30 stagflation trap.
